summary: Split a large monolithic assembly text file into several smaller asm files while preserving link order through ldscript.lcf. The PR moved roughly the early .text range starting at 0x80005940 out of asm/text.s into address-named files plus asm/lbvector.s, then explicitly listed those objects in the .text section before the remaining text.o.

key_files.role: source monolithic assembly file reduced by removing about 10400 lines that were split into new asm files
new split assembly file beginning at .text address 0x80005940; largest new chunk with 3676 added lines
new address-named split assembly file with 2621 added lines
new address-named split assembly file with 2132 added lines
new named assembly split, likely separated because this range had an identifiable library/module name
new address-named split assembly file with 356 added lines
updated .text output section to list split object files explicitly in order

what_changed: Added five new assembly files: asm/code_80005940.s, asm/code_80008D30.s, asm/code_8000B1CC.s, asm/lbvector.s, and asm/code_8000CE50.s.
Removed about 10400 lines from asm/text.s, indicating the old all-in-one assembly dump was partially carved into smaller files.
Changed ldscript.lcf from a wildcard .text section, `*(.text)`, to an explicit ordered list: code_80005940.o, code_80008D30.o, code_8000B1CC.o, code_8000CE50.o, lbvector.o, then text.o.

The new assembly chunks retain raw disassembly style with `.include "macros.inc"`, `.section .text, "ax"`, address comments, generated labels like lbl_80005A6C, and placeholder function names like func_80005B64.
The PR contains no body text, issue comments, review comments, or review records in the slice, so motivation and reviewer intent are not directly documented.
smart_moves: Preserved deterministic binary layout by replacing the broad .text wildcard with an explicit object order in ldscript.lcf after splitting the source files.
Used address-based filenames for unknown assembly regions, making each split chunk searchable and sortable by ROM/RAM address without inventing unsupported module names.
Kept the remaining asm/text.s linked after the extracted chunks, which lets the repository improve granularity incrementally without needing to name or decompile the entire text section at once.
Separated one chunk as lbvector.s rather than only using address names, showing that recognizable library/module boundaries can be named when evidence exists.
decomp_lessons: When splitting monolithic asm, update the linker script at the same time; otherwise object-file ordering may change and break matching.
For unidentified code, address-based filenames such as code_80005940.s are safer than speculative subsystem names.
A useful intermediate state in decompilation is not necessarily C code: smaller assembly files improve navigation, ownership, and future targeted decompilation.
Retaining original labels, global declarations, address comments, and SDA references during a file split minimizes semantic churn and helps confirm the move is layout-only.
If a file split introduces named modules like lbvector.s, keep stronger naming evidence for that module in later work; this PR itself does not document the rationale.
naming_conventions: Unknown assembly chunks are named `code_<start-address>.s`, preserving the uppercase hexadecimal style seen in files such as code_80008D30.s, code_8000B1CC.s, and code_8000CE50.s.
Generated functions continue to use `func_XXXXXXXX` names when no semantic name is established.
Generated local/global labels continue to use `lbl_XXXXXXXX` names.
Recognized library-like code may be given a semantic lowercase filename such as lbvector.s instead of a generic address filename.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicit linker-script ordering was used as the main matching tactic: the .text section lists each new object before text.o.
The split files preserve raw instruction comments with addresses and original opcodes, supporting byte-for-byte comparison.
The new files keep `.include "macros.inc"` and `.section .text, "ax"` declarations, matching the existing assembly-file pattern.
No evidence in the slice shows function-level decompilation, instruction rewriting, or semantic refactoring; the change appears to be an organizational assembly move.
review_feedback: No review comments, reviews, or issue comments were present in the PR slice.
No explicit reviewer guidance or requested changes can be extracted from the available evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or rename functions from asm/code_80005940.s.
Find later PRs involving asm/lbvector.s to confirm why that chunk was identified as lbvector.
Search for further ldscript.lcf changes that refine .text object ordering after additional asm splits.
Search for PRs replacing code_80008D30.s, code_8000B1CC.s, or code_8000CE50.s with C source files.
